refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation link availability at or above the customer-defined target over the agreed evaluation period.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR to explicitly state the performance metric (link availability) and the evaluation period clarifies the requirement for design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure link uptime and downtime over the evaluation period using telemetry data to verify availability meets or exceeds the target.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor teleoperation link status continuously.
FR2: Detect and report link failures promptly.
FR3: Recover teleoperation link to meet availability target.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement redundant network links with automatic failover.
DP2: Use link health monitoring and predictive maintenance algorithms.
DP3: Employ adaptive bandwidth management to prioritize teleoperation traffic.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement redundant network links with automatic failover.

potential_sources: Interaction with Safety Services for failover alerts
coupling_check.notes: Redundancy and failover logic are localized to network management, minimizing coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Risk of failover delay impacting link availability.
High safety criticality requires robust failover mechanisms.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating predictive monitoring (DP2) in future refinement to enhance availability.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical and embedded systems responsible for network link management and failover control.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Send link status and failover events to NGHE Solution and Safety Services.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for redundant network connections and failover switching.

module_containment_scores.notes: High-level coordination but not direct control of link redundancy.
Primary module for implementing network redundancy and failover.
Receives link status but does not control redundancy.
Monitors alerts but not involved in link management.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Network redundancy and failover control are best contained within the Vehicle module to minimize diffusion and ensure tight control over physical network interfaces.
containment_risks: Potential complexity in Vehicle module increasing integration risk.
Dependency on Vehicle hardware capabilities for redundancy.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Evaluate if some monitoring functions should be delegated to Teleoperation module to reduce Vehicle module complexity.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP (redundant network links with failover) directly addresses the refined FR of maintaining link availability.

diffusion_risks: Some diffusion risk if monitoring functions spread across Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment within Vehicle module is strong for failover control, minimizing diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification via telemetry data and failover event logs is feasible within assigned IM boundaries.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility for link availability to Vehicle module.

recommended_revisions: Consider refining monitoring sub-FRs and possibly assigning monitoring DP/IM to Teleoperation for modularity.
Clarify interface protocols for failover events between Vehicle and Safety Services.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, further refinement of monitoring functions and interface clarity is needed to reduce diffusion and improve modularity.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor teleoperation link status continuously.
FR2: Detect and report link failures promptly.
FR3: Recover teleoperation link to meet availability target.

revise.why: To consider splitting monitoring functions into Teleoperation module and clarify interface definitions to reduce diffusion risk.

assumptions: Network infrastructure supports redundancy and failover.
Vehicle module has capability to implement failover control.
evidence_needed: Interface specifications between Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services.
